questionHtml:
The following compounds have similar relative molecular masses. What is the order of increasing boiling point?


A.  CH3CH2CH2OH < CH3CH2CHO < CH3COOH

B.  CH3CH2CHO < CH3CH2CH2OH < CH3COOH

C.  CH3CH2CHO < CH3COOH < CH3CH2CH2OH

D.  CH3COOH < CH3CH2CHO < CH3CH2CH2OH

meta.title: The following compounds have similar relative molecular masses. What is the order of increasing boiling point? A. CH3CH2CH2OH < CH3CH2CHO < CH3COOH B. CH3CH2CHO < CH3CH2CH2OH < CH3
breadcrumbLabels: Structure 2. Models of bonding and structure

Structure 2.2.9—Given comparable molar mass, the relative strengths of intermolecular forces are generally: London (dispersion) forces < dipole–dipole forces < hydrogen bonding. Explain the physical properties of covalent substances to include volatility, electrical conductivity and solubility in terms of their structure.
